title: ABI, Shared Libraries, and Foreign-Function Memory · Object-Lifetime Lens: Unwind Error Boundary
summary: A C library shares opaque handles and buffers with Rust, Python, and WebAssembly consumers that use different allocators and unwind rules. Planned artifact: Add and verify the cmem unwind error boundary increment in versioned cross-language ownership ABI. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Convert errors to stable status and out-value contracts and prevent language exceptions or panics from crossing unsupported C frames.
Pass buffers with lengths, ownership direction, allocator family, and release callback across FFI.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Unwind Error Boundary · Theory Unwind Error Boundary
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory native-systems team handles memory case 7874. Produce bounded C evidence to validate a versioned foreign buffer and allocator family; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset.
